CASTROL INDIA Q4 : RECORD VOLUME GROWTH & ₹4,000+ CR FY26 REVENUE 🛢️📈

• 💰 Standalone Q4 revenue hit all-time high of ₹1,040 Cr, growing 13.7% YoY

• 📊 FY26 consolidated revenue crossed ₹4,000 Cr, up 11.7% YoY

• 🏭 FY26 EBITDA crossed ₹500 Cr milestone

• 📈 Q4 standalone EBITDA stood at ₹135 Cr with 13.0% margin despite elevated crude prices & rupee weakness

• 💪 PBT (before exceptional items) excluding prior-year land sale impact grew 4% in FY26

• 🛢️ Crude oil spike and rupee depreciation increased raw material & imported input costs

• ⚙️ Margin pressure managed through pricing actions, cost optimization & operational agility

• 🚀 Lubricants volume grew 14% YoY in Q4, over 3x industry growth, marking record quarterly volume

• 📦 FY26 volume growth stood at 10.5%

• 🔄 Growth remained broad-based across B2C, OEM and B2B segments

• 🌾 Strong gains seen in Agri, PCMO, MCO and CVO segments

• 🏗️ B2B Industrial, Infrastructure & Mining segments delivered high double-digit growth

reports.text:
PG ELECTROPLAST Q4 ; Company continues to invest in future growth and backward integration across key product categories. 

It is developing a new refrigerator manufacturing plant in Sri City, targeted for commissioning by 4QFY27, and is also setting up a rotary compressor manufacturing facility at Supa to strengthen backward integration in the Room AC business, with operations expected to commence by 4QFY27
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
PG ELECTROPLAST Q4 : ROOM AC BUSINESS HIT BY DEMAND & SUPPLY DISRUPTIONS ❄️⚠️

• 📉 Q4FY26 remained challenging due to weak Room AC demand and multiple supply-side disruptions

• 🏬 Elevated channel inventory after BEE transition impacted pricing power in Jan-Feb 2026

• 💸 Commodity inflation & rupee depreciation caused ~250 bps gross margin impact

• ⛽ LPG shortage due to Gulf conflict disrupted March production, impacting Room AC output worth ~₹300 Cr

• 🚚 Truck availability issues caused additional estimated sales loss of ~₹120 Cr

• 📊 Combined disruptions led to aggregate revenue loss of ~₹420 Cr and PBT impact of ~₹120 Cr during the quarter

• 💱 Forex loss stood at ₹38.77 Cr in FY26 vs forex gain of ₹17.99 Cr last year

• 💰 Q4 consolidated revenue came at ₹1,717 Cr; product business contributed 82.2%

• ❄️ Room AC revenue declined 12% YoY to ₹1,210 Cr

• 🧺 Washing machine business grew 70% YoY, while cooler business declined 10.8% YoY

• 📺 Electronics business contributed 6.8% of quarterly revenue

• 🤝 Goodworth Electronics JV revenue rose to ₹155.1 Cr vs ₹107.6 Cr YoY; EBITDA improved sharply to ₹6.19 Cr
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:

reports.text:
ADVAIT ENERGY FORMS NEW BESS SUBSIDIARY FOR ENERGY STORAGE BUSINESS 🔋⚡

• Incorporated new subsidiary “Advait BESS Bhesaan Private Limited”

• New entity will focus on Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) and renewable energy storage solutions

• Business scope includes EPC/turnkey projects, engineering, integration, testing, commissioning and maintenance services

• Company will support captive, grid-connected and off-grid renewable energy storage applications

• Subsidiary will also undertake leasing, operation and infrastructure support services for BESS projects

• Initial authorised share capital stands at ₹1 lakh

• Registered office located in Ahmedabad, Gujarat

• Advait Energy will hold 51% stake in the subsidiary

PATEL ENGINEERING : JV BLACKLISTED BY NEPAL AUTHORITY FOR HYDRO PROJECT ⚠️🏗️

• 🇳🇵 Public Procurement Monitoring Office, Government of Nepal blacklisted Raman Patel JV for 27.05.2026 to 26.05.2029

• 🏞️ Blacklisting relates to Sunkoshi Marin Diversion Multipurpose Project Lot-1 in Nepal

• 🤝 Patel Engineering held 35% stake in JV; Raman Construction was lead partner with 65%

• 📄 Disputes arose between JV and employer over contractual obligations, leading to contract termination

• ⚖️ Company stated disputes are under adjudication and matter remains sub-judice

• 💬 Patel Engineering says all project execution activities were under domain of lead partner Raman

• 📊 Company clarified there is no material impact on its financials and operations

🔴 Impact: Negative (Regulatory blacklisting may impact reputation and future project participation sentiment despite limited direct financial impact claim)

• 🇮🇳 Khavda project remains one of India’s major renewable energy developments

reports.text:
Magellanic Cloud Limited Posts Landmark FY26 Results with ₹700+ Cr Total Income, Alongside Strong Q4 Performance with ₹205.5 Cr Revenue and 8% EBITDA Growth

📌FY26 Consolidated Performance Highlights
- Revenue from Operations stood at ₹697.9 Cr, registering a growth of 16.9% YoY. 
- Total Income increased to ₹706.8 Cr, reflecting a growth of 17.5% YoY. 
- Total Expenditure stood at ₹559.0 Cr, up 21.9% YoY. 
- EBITDA stood at ₹224.3 Cr, registering a growth of 7.0% YoY. 
- EBITDA Margin stood at 32.1%, compared to 35.1% in FY25. 
- Depreciation expense stood at ₹50.8 Cr, reflecting a growth of 19.6% YoY. 
- Finance Cost stood at ₹25.7 Cr, registering a growth of 5.0% YoY. 
- Profit Before Tax (PBT) stood at ₹147.8 Cr, reflecting a growth of 3.6% YoY. 
- Profit After Tax (PAT) stood at ₹114.4 Cr, registering a growth of 11.4% YoY. 

📌Q4 FY26 Consolidated Performance Highlights

- Profit Before Tax (PBT) stood at ₹35.5 Cr in Q4 FY26 as compared to ₹30.5 Cr in Q4 FY25, while registering a growth of 2.4% QoQ. 
- Profit After Tax (PAT) stood at ₹30.4 Cr in Q4 FY26 as compared to ₹22.6 Cr in Q4 FY25, while registering a growth of 6.1% QoQ.

SANSTAR Q4 : RECOVERY MOMENTUM & CAPACITY EXPANSION DRIVE OUTLOOK 🌽⚙️

• 🔄 FY26 remained a transition year impacted by maintenance shutdowns, lower utilization & pricing pressure from Chinese native starch exports

• 📈 H2FY26 witnessed gradual recovery in operations, utilization & profitability

• 💰 FY26 Revenue from Operations stood at ₹784.6 Cr

• 📊 Gross Profit Margin improved sharply to 30.1% vs 25.6% YoY due to better efficiencies & product mix

• ⚡ Q4FY26 Revenue grew 7.4% QoQ to ₹216.8 Cr

• 🏭 Q4 EBITDA rose 8.2% QoQ to ₹19.4 Cr

• 💵 Q4 PAT stood at ₹20.5 Cr with PAT margin improving to 9.5% vs 6.8% QoQ

• 🌍 Export revenue for FY26 came at ₹265.7 Cr despite weak global starch pricing & geopolitical uncertainties

• 🏗️ Successfully expanded Dhule native starch capacity to 1,250 TPD vs initial 1,000 TPD IPO plan

• 🥈 Became India’s second-largest maize-based specialty products manufacturer with total installed capacity of 2,350 TPD

• 🧪 Derivatives facility at Dhule expected commissioning in FY27 to increase value-added product contribution

• 💸 Company incurred ~₹225 Cr capex to enhance maize grinding & manufacturing scale

• 📦 Management expects improved product diversification, utilization & margin stability in FY27 with derivatives ramp-up

NLC INDIA : AGGRESSIVE TRANSFORMATION INTO CLEAN ENERGY PLATFORM ⚡🌱

• 🔄 NLC India transitioning from a thermal-focused utility to a diversified energy platform

• 🇮🇳 Strategy aligned with India’s COP26 & Panchamrit clean energy commitments

• ☀️ Company targeting 10.11 GW renewable energy capacity by FY30

• ⚡ Renewable energy share in generation mix targeted at 50% by 2030

• 💰 Planned renewable energy investment of ~₹50,000 Cr

• 🌍 Company targeting ~16 MT CO₂ offset by 2030

• 🏗️ Renewable capacity already scaled from 491 MW in FY17 to 1,734 MW in FY26

• 📈 Renewable capacity pipeline targeted to rise to 10,110 MW by FY30

• 🔋 Diversification underway into energy storage, critical minerals & green hydrogen

• 🚫 Management committed to no new conventional fossil-fuel assets beyond 2030

• 🌱 Clean power generation targeted to increase from 3.06 billion units currently to 17.2 billion units

• 🏠 Green energy impact expected to power ~1.5 crore homes in future vs current 27 lakh homes

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strong renewable expansion roadmap, clean energy diversification and large investment pipeline position NLC India for long-term energy transition growth)

GOLDIAM LAUNCHES INDIA’S FIRST DIGITAL 3D RING BUILDER 💍🖥️

• 💎 ORIGEM launched India’s first indigenous web-based Digital 3D Ring Builder platform

• 🌐 Platform allows customers to configure, visualize and order bespoke diamond rings in interactive 3D format

• 🔄 Users can rotate, zoom and customize rings across desktop, tablet & smartphone devices

• 💍 Customers can choose from 6 ring styles, 9 centre-stone shapes, 6 stone sizes, multiple side-stone configurations & engraving options

• 🟡 Platform supports metal customization across 10/14K white, yellow & rose gold variants

• 🛍️ Company aims to strengthen retail expansion and customer engagement through tech-led jewellery buying experience

• 📈 Management highlighted growing B2B bridal jewellery strength and plans to expand into high-ASP core jewellery categories

• 🏬 ORIGEM expected to build strong retail footprint with target of becoming among India’s largest LGD jewellery brands

• 🔬 Company also focusing on Gold Exchange, payment schemes and VVS quality diamond lines to drive customer trust and sales

SIMPLEX CASTINGS RECEIVES RDSO APPROVAL FOR CAST STEEL BOGIES 🚆⚙️

• ✅ Simplex Castings received RDSO approval for prototype testing of Cast Steel CASNUB Bogie and critical railway wagon components

• 🛠️ Approval covers components including Cast Steel Side Frames, Bolsters, Centre Pivot Assemblies & CASNUB 22HS/22HSM/22RFT bogies

• 🚄 Company aims to tap expanding railway modernization and freight corridor opportunities

• 📈 Management highlighted active bidding for railway tenders following approval

• 🏭 Manufacturing facility undergoing capex to support bogie production at scale

• 💰 Railway business expected to generate ~₹50 Cr revenue by end of FY27

• 🚀 Company targets railway segment revenue of ₹100+ Cr by end of next financial year

reports.text:
Rikhav Securities Limited Reports EBITDA of ₹32.53 Cr & PAT of ₹18.96 Cr in FY26

Rikhav Securities Limited, one of the leading diversified stock market services providers has announced its Audited Financial Results for H2 & FY26.

H2 FY26 Financial Highlights
* Total Income of ₹ 1604.95 Cr
* EBITDA of ₹ 6.57 Cr
* PAT of ₹ 1.20 Cr
* EPS of ₹ 0.31

FY26 Financial Highlights
* Total Income of ₹ 1991.62 Cr
* EBITDA of ₹ 32.53 Cr
* PAT of ₹ 18.96 Cr
* EPS of ₹ 4.95

Financial & Business Updates FY26
- Invested ₹5.40 Cr in technology infrastructure and software during FY26, leading to higher depreciation
- Profitability impacted by ₹22.93 Cr loss from share trading activities
- Unrealised SLBM profit of ₹4.33 Cr not recognised in FY26 financials and expected to be realised in upcoming quarter
- Current liabilities increased mainly due to SLBM borrowings of ₹47.34 Cr

Currently, the stock is trading at ₹47.40, with a Mcap of 181.50 Cr. (As on 23rd May 2026)

Top fund managers have very different takes on Indian IT.

Rajeev Thakkar is finding value at current levels and is buying IT across funds.

Prashant Jain is neutral. His view the sector may need 2 more years for better clarity.

Kenneth Andrade has completely exited IT, citing uncertainty around the real impact of AI on the sector.

S. Naren, the contrarian, is also seeing value and continues to hold IT stocks.

Samir Arora is bearish on traditional IT, with zero allocation there, but prefers select tech and capital market plays.

Sunil Singhania has no exposure to traditional IT stocks, but is buying a product-based tech company.

It will be interesting to see who gets this right.

Indian Railways’ ₹40,000Cr Wagon Tender Signals Multi-Year Demand Visibility

Indian Railways plans ₹40,000Cr freight wagon procurement over 3–4 years.

Tender size targets nearly 100,000 wagons, exceeding the 2022 procurement cycle.

Annual procurement could reach 35,000–40,000 wagons under phased ordering structure.

First tranche of orders likely during July–September quarter this fiscal year.

Initiative aims to absorb expanded manufacturing capacities across domestic wagon makers.

Previous 2022 tender worth ₹32,000Cr also covered roughly 100,000 wagons.

Pace Digitek is building FY27–FY28 growth visibility around BESS-led manufacturing scale-up. ⚡

The company plans to expand BESS capacity from 2.5 GWh to 10 GWh in phases starting from Q2/Q3FY27, with new facilities and in-house container fabrication aimed at improving cost efficiency, delivery timelines and margin profile.

Order book stands at ₹11,338 cr, diversified across BESS, telecom, OFC and digital infrastructure projects.

Revenue guidance:
FY27E: ₹3,200–3,400 cr
FY28E: ₹4,000–4,200 cr
FY26: ₹2641 Cr

The opportunity is large, but the real monitor will be execution — timely commissioning, order book conversion and margin protection while scaling.

Patel Retail Reports Strong FY26 Performance; PAT Surges 54% with Margin Expansion of 63 Bps

Patel Retail Limited, a diversified retail and food processing company has announced its audited financial results for Q4 & FY26.

FY26 Financial Highlights
Total Income of ₹ 1,059.29 Cr, YoY growth of 28.25%
EBITDA of ₹ 83.08 Cr, YoY growth of 33.07%
EBITDA Margin of 7.84%, YoY expansion of 28 Bps
PAT of ₹ 39.05 Cr, YoY growth of 54.48%
PAT Margin of 3.69%, YoY expansion of 63 Bps
EPS of ₹ 13.03, YoY growth of 26.50%

Q4 FY26 Financial Highlights
Total Income of ₹ 339.55 Cr, YoY growth of 53.35%
EBITDA of ₹ 22.74 Cr, YoY growth of 31.21%
EBITDA Margin of 6.70%, YoY decline of (113) Bps
PAT of ₹ 9.98 Cr, YoY growth of 39.07%
PAT Margin of 2.94%, YoY decline of (30) Bps
EPS of ₹ 2.99, YoY growth of 3.82%

Q4 FY26 Operational Highlights
Retail Expansion – During Q4 FY26, the company launched its 50th store in Thakurli and 51st store in Rasayani, strengthening its retail footprint across the Mumbai Metropolitan Region (MMR).

Export Authorization – Received DGFT approval for export of wheat flour and related products, strengthening international market presence and export capabilities.

Captain Polyplast Limited - Q4 & FY26 Concall Highlights

Company Overview
Captain Polyplast Limited has been a leading player in the Micro Irrigation Systems (MIS) industry for nearly three decades. The Company has strategically diversified into the Solar EPC segment, focusing on solar water pumping systems and rooftop solar solutions. Backed by a strong distribution network of over 750 dealers across key agricultural states, the Company provides integrated solutions along with robust after-sales support to farmers and customers.

Financial Performance

Q4 FY26 Performance
* Reported its highest-ever quarterly revenue of ₹142 Cr, registering an 80% YoY growth from ₹79.1 Cr in Q4 FY25.
* EBITDA increased 66% YoY to ₹14.16 Cr.
* EBITDA Margin stood at 9.96%.
* Net Profit surged 91% YoY to ₹9.76 Cr, while EPS stood at ₹1.64.

FY26 Performance
* Total Income reached ₹419 Cr, reflecting a robust 44% YoY growth over FY25.
* Full-year EBITDA stood at ₹46.32 Cr with an EBITDA Margin of 11.03%.

Revenue Mix & Profitability
Business Segment Mix
* Current revenue mix stands at approximately 80:20 / 85:15 (MIS : Solar EPC).
* Management aims to achieve a 50:50 revenue mix within the next two years.

Growing Non-Subsidy Contribution
* The cash (non-subsidy) segment contributed 15% of MIS revenues during FY26.
* The rooftop solar business operates entirely on a cash-and-carry model.

Profitability Outlook
Management highlighted that solar pump margins remain structurally aligned with MIS margins, indicating no margin dilution from the evolving business mix.

Operations & Expansion
Ahmedabad Facility & Backward Integration
* The new 70,000 sq. ft. Ahmedabad manufacturing facility is now operational.
* The facility manufactures high-margin micro irrigation components in-house.
* Manufacturing Capacity. Combined capacity across Rajkot, Kurnool, and Ahmedabad plants can support up to ₹400 Cr MIS revenue without any major additional capex.

Solar Pumps Business Momentum
* Executed 1,500 solar pumps in Maharashtra during the first year of operations.
* Secured a fresh 500-pump order worth ₹11 Cr.

Raw Material & Margin Trends
Q4 profitability was impacted by a sharp increase in raw material prices during March 2026, primarily due to unforeseen geopolitical developments. This remained the key reason behind the 86 bps margin contraction.

Management Guidance & Growth Outlook
* Management is targeting 20–25% CAGR growth in the MIS segment over the next three years, primarily driven by market share gains.
* The Solar EPC segment is expected to grow at a faster pace to gradually match MIS revenues.

Export Strategy
* Exports currently contribute around 5% of MIS revenue.
* Focus remains on drip irrigation products for African and Latin American markets.
* Management targets double-digit export contribution within the next five years.

**MINDA CORPORATION – PREMIUM ELECTRONICS & EV GROWTH MOMENTUM**  

**Brokerage View**
- Axis Securities maintained BUY rating
- Target price set at ₹710
- Implies 23% upside from CMP ₹575
- Valuation based on 28x FY28E EPS

**Order Book Strength**
- Lifetime order book crossed ₹10,000 Cr
- Q4FY26 new wins worth ~₹3,500 Cr
- Around 20% orders linked to exports
- Strong visibility across 48–60 month execution cycle

**Premium & EV Expansion**
- Won lifetime sunroof order worth ~₹1,050 Cr
- Mass production expected within 4–5 months
- Expanded TFT cluster portfolio from 3-inch to 15-inch displays
- EV focus strengthened through Turntide Technologies JV

**Technology & Innovation**
- Filed 11 new patents during Q4FY26
- Total patent filings reached 330
- 147 patents already granted
- Over 950 engineers across global R&D centres

**Export & Global Growth**
- Export-led strategy gaining momentum
- Facilities in Vietnam & Indonesia supporting expansion
- Pune die-casting plant positioned as export hub
- Diversifying beyond domestic auto cycle

**Flash Electronics Performance**
- Associate Flash Electronics revenue at ~₹493 Cr
- EBITDA surged 106% YoY to ₹89 Cr
- PAT increased 137% YoY to ₹49 Cr
- Export programs and EV portfolio driving growth

**Q4FY26 Performance**
- Revenue grew 29% YoY to ₹1,704 Cr
- EBITDA increased 33% YoY to ₹203 Cr
- EBITDA margin improved to 11.9%
- PAT jumped 138% YoY to ₹124 Cr

**Margin & Operating Leverage**
- EBITDA margins guided above 11%
- Premiumisation improving profitability
- Automation & AI-driven efficiencies supporting margins
- Commodity inflation managed through pass-through mechanisms

**Capex & Capacity Expansion**
- FY26 capex stood at record ₹413 Cr
- FY27 capex guidance at ₹400–450 Cr

Indian EV purchases are increasingly being driven by arithmetic, not aspiration.🧮

The Indian EV transition is increasingly being pulled by economics, not only pushed by policy. A cumulative ₹5/litre rise in petrol and diesel in May has made running-cost savings far more tangible for retail customers and fleets.

The illustrative math in the ₹11–12 lakh segment: EVs cost more upfront, but their 5-year running cost is far lower than petrol, diesel, or CNG. That is why EVs are increasingly being treated as a financial hedge, in addition to being a green choice. 

The market is already responding. EV PV retail share rose to 5.77% in April 2026 from 3.70% a year earlier.

EVs may still face friction on model choice, charging convenience, and resale value, to be addressed. 

The implication for industry is significant: product positioning, financing, and resale support will matter as much as battery specs. In a market like India, ownership economics will decide scale.

The company also announced an investment of **INR 200 crore** for setting up **GPIL BESS**.

4Q broadly in line with estimates (+2% vs. GSe), with sales/EBITDA growing by +6%/+1% yoy driven by strong growth in Europe (+29% yoy) and growth markets (+25% yoy) partially offset by a slower US which is still seeing impacts of the gRevlimid ramp-down (-13% yoy).

EBITDA margin came in steady at 20.3% (-16bps vs. GSe), as higher GMs (+216bps yoy) were offset by higher employee costs

Co aspires to achieve high single digit topline growth (ex-gRevlimid) while guiding to maintain margins at 21%+ mark in FY27

@Stockupdate9

Nuvama on Aurobindo
Buy, TP Rs 1680

Reported in-line revenue/EBITDA, but a 5% miss on PAT
Gross margin was better than estimate, while EBTDA margin was broadly in-line

Weak US growth was offset by strong growth in Europe and growth markets

Remain optimistic on Aurobindo’s growth/margin prospects as the company is nearing an inflection point of operating leverage in Pen-G and TheraNym CMO unit.

Aurobindo is also set to benefit from growth in Europe and Lannett closure

@Stockupdate9

DAM Cap on Aurobindo
Buy, TP Rs 1767

Co’s steady operating performance over multiple qtrs. reinforces view that it is best placed to any leverage generic industry tailwinds.

Aurobindo is stepping on gas with growing investments in biosimilars, peptides, injectables, etc.

Innovative biologics CMO for MSD adds another medium-term growth engine.

Recent Lannett acquisition seems to be another value accretive M&A

Anticipate meaningful impact of recent big-ticket investments (PenG, China, granulation, injectables) to be visible in coming quarters along with capex intensity easing.

Nikkei briefly dipped under 60k as AI and semiconductor stocks crashed. JPM says it’s a technical overheating issue, not a fundamental economic crisis. A short-term cooling off.

JPM’s Z-score model shows momentum surged to +3.90, then crashed to -5.30. Expect momentum to stay dead for 1 month. Smart move? Rotate money into classic Value stocks.

Regional banks are the ultimate hedge right now. They mix cheap Value with steady Momentum. Plus, they win big as the BOJ stays hawkish and hikes interest rates from June.

🎯 JPM Summary: Don't panic dump. Tech is just cooling down. Lock your capital into Japanese regional banks. They offer zero pain trades and clear profits from rising interest rates.
